steps.title: Extract relevant counts from the table
steps.content: From the table: grade 10 on‑campus = 30, grade 10 off‑campus = 18, grade 11 on‑campus = 33, grade 11 off‑campus = 16.

steps.title: Compute conditional probability
steps.content:
First, find the total number of students in grades 10 and 11: $$(30+18)+(33+16)=48+49=97$$.
Next, find how many of those stayed on campus: $$(30+33)=63$$.
The conditional probability is $$\frac{63}{97}\approx0.6495$$, which rounds to 0.649.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
